text: Assistant: Both fixed and validated. On the modal — you were seeing a **cached `panel.js`** (the old one, without the skip), so the sidebar kept firing. I didn't just patch the skip; I made the modal *win regardless*: `essay.js` now handles the click in the **capture phase** and stops the event before any sidebar handler can run, and I swapped the fragile iframe for **inline essay content**. A fresh-cache browser test now confirms: clicking Trellis opens a **centered "Receipts." essay modal** (not the sidebar), it scrolls the full essay, closes via Esc / × / backdrop, and reads cleanly in dark mode — zero console errors. Since your view still showed the sidebar, you're on a cached tab or the deployed site: **hard-refresh** `/stuff/`, or say the word and I'll deploy.
